LAW AND MOTION 301, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - COUT MODIFIS ITS TENATATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND ITS RECREATION AND PARKS GENERAL MANAGER PHIL GINSBURG'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED. COURT GRANTS LEAVE TO AMEND. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING) COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. ONCE RECEIVED, COURT WILL MODIFY THE SUBMITTED PROPOSED ORDER TO REFLECT LEAVE TO AMEND IS GRANTED. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: W. DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-11-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
DAVID G. HOLLENBERG, ESQ., GOETHALS & STONE, PC (650) 218-4319 dave@gostlaw.com Attorney for Plaintiff

SAMUEL A. LEFF, ESQ. Deputy City Attorney(415) 554-3923 samuel.leff@sfcityatty.org Attorney for Defendants CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO and PHIL GINSBURG.

Raquel Sharp, CSR#10619 raquel@transcriptlegal.com, 925-954-9888 Court reporter,

Hearing is held. Discussed, not ARGUED. The Court modifies and adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, PHIL GINSBURG'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Defendants the City and County of San Francisco and its Recreation and Parks general manager Phil Ginsburg's demurrer to plaintiffs' complaint is sustained. Court grants leave to amend.

(SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling. Once received, Court will modify the submitted proposed order to reflect leave to amend is granted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Raquel Sharp, CSR#10619 raquel@transcriptlegal.com, 925-954-9888 Reported (301/CVA)
